children.heading: Recipient countries
children.label: 1 Recipient countries
children.text: A proposal to enter into a nonemergency food assistance agreement under this subchapter shall identify the recipient country or countries that are the subject of the agreement.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 2 Timing
children.text: Not later than 120 days after the date of receipt by the Administrator of a proposal submitted by an eligible organization under this subchapter, the Administrator shall determine whether to accept the proposal.

children.heading: Denial
children.label: 3 Denial
children.text: If a proposal under paragraph (1) is denied, the response shall specify the reasons for denial.

provide notice of the existence of a proposed guideline or annual policy guidance, and that such guideline or annual policy guidance is available for review and comment, to eligible organizations that participate in programs under this subchapter, and to other interested persons;
make the proposed guideline or annual policy guidance available, on request, to the eligible organizations and other persons referred to in paragraph (1); and
take any comments received into consideration prior to the issuance of the final guideline or annual policy guidance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall promptly issue all necessary regulations and make revisions to agency guidelines with respect to changes in the operation or implementation of the program established under this subchapter. Not later than 270 days after the date of the enactment of the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018, the Administrator shall issue all regulations and revisions to agency guidance necessary to implement the amendments made to this subchapter by such Act.

children.label: A
children.text: simplifying procedures for participation in the programs established under this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: reducing paperwork requirements under such programs;

children.label: C
children.text: establishing reasonable and realistic accountability standards to be applied to eligible organizations participating in the programs established under this subchapter, taking into consideration the problems associated with carrying out programs in developing countries; and

children.label: D
children.text: providing flexibility for carrying out programs under this subchapter.

children.heading: Timely provision of commodities
children.label: d Timely provision of commodities
children.text: The Administrator, in consultation with the Secretary, shall develop procedures that ensure expedited processing of commodity call forwards in order to provide commodities overseas in a timely manner and to the extent feasible, according to planned delivery schedules.

children.heading: Timely approval
children.label: e Timely approval
children.text: The Administrator is encouraged to finalize program agreements and resource requests for programs under this section before the beginning of each fiscal year.

children.heading: Program oversight, monitoring, and evaluation
children.label: f Program oversight, monitoring, and evaluation
A
children.text: to determine the need for assistance provided under this subchapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: to improve, monitor, and evaluate the effectiveness and efficiency of the assistance provided under this subchapter to maximize the impact of the assistance.

children.label: A
children.text: program monitors in countries that receive assistance under this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: country and regional food aid impact evaluations;

children.label: C
children.text: the identification and implementation of best practices for food aid programs;

children.label: D
children.text: the evaluation of monetization programs;

children.label: E
children.text: early warning assessments and systems to help prevent famines; and

children.label: F
children.text: maintenance of information technology systems.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), in carrying out administrative and management activities relating to each activity carried out by the Administrator under paragraph (1), the Administrator may enter into contracts with 1 or more individuals for personal service to be performed in recipient countries or neighboring countries.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: B Prohibition
children.text: An individual who enters into a contract with the Administrator under subparagraph (A) shall not be considered to be an employee of the Federal Government for the purpose of any law (including regulations) administered by the Office of Personnel Management.

children.heading: Personal service
children.label: C Personal service
children.text: Subparagraph (A) does not limit the ability of the Administrator to enter into a contract with any individual for personal service under .section 1722(a) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to , in addition to other funds made available to the Administrator to carry out the monitoring of emergency food assistance, the Administrator may implement this subsection using up to 1.5 percent, but not less than $17,000,000, of the funds made available under this subchapter for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023, except for paragraph (2)(F), for which not more than $500,000 shall be made available for each of the fiscal years 2014 through 2023.section 1722(h)(3) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), of the funds made available under subparagraph (A), for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2023, not more than $8,000,000 may be used by the Administrator to carry out paragraph (2)(E).

children.heading: Condition
children.label: ii Condition
children.text: No funds shall be made available under subparagraph (A), in accordance with clause (i), unless not less than $8,000,000 is made available under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.) for such purposes for such fiscal year.22 U.S.C. 2151

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In submitting project reports to the Administrator, a private voluntary organization or cooperative shall provide a copy of the report in such form as is necessary for the report to be displayed for public use on the website of the United States Agency for International Development.

children.heading: Confidential information
children.label: 2 Confidential information
children.text: An organization or cooperative described in paragraph (1) may omit any confidential information from the copy of the report submitted for public display under that paragraph.
